evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: objetivo-metodo-hallazgo-general
evidence_units.original_text: gas (GHG) emissions by 2030 compared to 1990 levels and to be a low-emission society by 2050. Given that 85–90% of today’s buildings are expected to still be in use in 2050, refurbishment and adaptive reuse of existing buildings can help in achieving the environmental goals. The aim of this work is to provide a holistic picture of refurbishment and adaptive reuse of existing buildings, including buildings with heritage values, seen from a life cycle perspective. The methods applied are a literature review of LCA studies and experiences from quantitative case study analysis of selected Norwegian case studies. The ﬁndings show that extending the service life of existing buildings by refurbishment and adaptive reuse has signiﬁcant possibilities in reducing GHG emissions, keeping cultural heritage values, and saving scarce raw material resources. The ﬁndings show limited LCA studies, uncertainties in existing LCA studies due to variations in case-speciﬁc refurbishment or inter- vention measures, and a lack of transparent and harmonized background data and methodological choices. In conclusion, performing a holistic study covering the whole LCA and including socio- cultural values and economic aspects will enable supporting an argument to assert the sustainability of existing buildings. Keywords: embodied emission; existing building; LCA; heritage value; socio-cultural value 1. Introduction Adopted by 195 countries in December 2015, the Paris Agreement was a major step
evidence_units.summary_es: Resume de forma directa el objetivo del artículo, el método empleado y el hallazgo principal sobre reducción de emisiones y conservación de valores culturales. Es un chunk base para antecedentes, justificación y marco metodológico.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: lineas-futuras-holismo-economia-social
evidence_units.original_text: Appl. Sci. 2021, 11, 5978 16 of 19 with historic/heritage values. This can also support incorporating refurbishment and adaptive reuse projects in the on-going work on setting national GHG emis- sion requirements and reference or benchmark values in Norwegian building codes (TEK) [62]. • The scope of the current LCA studies is limited to environmental impact assess- ment. Further studies should consider incorporating the economic, social, and his- toric/heritage aspects to widen the scope of LCA and provide a more holistic view. It is important to include other indicators such as resource depletion and toxicity to avoid problem shifting from one indicator to another. In addition, incorporating the dynamic LCA approach will provide a better understanding of time aspects of refurbishment and adaptive reuse. • Investigation of potential environmental, economic, and social strains and beneﬁts by using a dynamic input–output analysis method is essential. This enables evaluating the supply chain effects caused by the current and future changes in demand and technological developments at different levels (building, neighborhood, city, region, country, global). • Activities related to refurbishment and adaptive reuse of existing buildings, with envi- ronmental and social beneﬁts proven by LCA studies, should receive ﬁnancial support, incentives, and subsidies to support and emphasize the role of historic/heritage build- ings in climate change mitigation. 6. Conclusions
evidence_units.summary_es: Expone con claridad las líneas futuras: ampliar el alcance ambiental hacia economía, aspectos sociales y patrimoniales, incorporar otros indicadores y enfoque dinámico. Es clave para justificar un marco de factibilidad más integral.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: lca-como-herramienta-decision-y-escenarios
evidence_units.original_text: user behavior should also be taken into consideration. Further studies should perform scenario analyses to evaluate different realistic refurbishment measures addressing the uncertainties in background data, assumptions, and methodological choices. Creating awareness and developing expertise to ﬁll in the knowledge gap in this area are also needed. • A life cycle assessment should be used as a decision support tool to assess the sus- tainability of refurbishment measures, assess the performance before and after refur- bishment, and make informed decisions regarding refurbishment vs. demolition and new building scenarios. Further studies should consider performing detailed and transparent LCA of existing buildings, including both refurbishment and adaptive reuse of existing buildings with historic/heritage values. The study should provide a clear description of the ambitions (for, e.g., achieving TEK 17, ZEB, passive house, plus house) and scope of the study (for, e.g., level of refurbishment, LCA system boundaries following EN 15978 or NS 3720, building physical boundaries in accordance with NS 3451) including the whole life cycle of the building (modules A1–D). Cultural heritage values and different environmental indicators than GHG emissions should also be considered in the analysis. • Implementing refurbishment and adaptive reuse measures to achieve reduced emis- sion values can be difﬁcult without affecting the heritage values. This calls for a
evidence_units.summary_es: Ofrece una recomendación aplicable: usar LCA como herramienta de apoyo a decisiones entre rehabilitación y demolición/nueva construcción, incorporando análisis de escenarios y transparencia. Muy valioso para una tesis aplicada.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: genius_of_place_y_sitio_especifico
evidence_units.original_text: 117 objective is the we llbeing of ecosystems and people, rather than ‘power, prestige or profit’. Ecomimicry is a kind of biomimicry where flora and fauna of a particular place are studied to find technologies or m ethods that will fit best to the unique conditions of the site. This is because they are most likely to be optimally suited to a particular place because they have evolved to work within a given set of climatic conditions and local resource constraints (Marshall, 2007b) . This is similar to the ‘Genius of Place’ idea fro m the Biomimicry Guild where local biology or ecology is studied to find solutions for site specific design challenges (Peters, 2011, Biomimicry Guild, 2011). Other design ideas or strategies related to ecosystems and architecture are discussed in chapter three. The most important advantage of such an approach to biomimetic design may be the potential positive effects on overall environmental performance. Bio-inspired buildings are likely to have better sustainability outcomes if they mimic the process strategies (how they work) and/or the functions (what they do) of ecosystems, rather than simply the form or material properties of individual organisms (Reap et al., 2005). Ecosystem b iomimicry can operate at both a metaphoric level and a functional level. At a metaphoric level, general ecosystem processes (based on how most ecosystems work) are able to be applied by designers with little ecological
evidence_units.summary_es: Muy útil para criterios de adecuación al sitio y lectura contextual. El concepto de Genius of Place puede traducirse a evaluación de compatibilidad local en reconversión y densificación, aunque la fuente no trate Toronto ni vivienda.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: principios_ecosistemicos_y_matriz
evidence_units.original_text: 146 The methodology used to devise the generalised ecosystem principles is described, and each principle is briefly elaborated. The results are presented as both a simplified list and an organisational matrix diagram where the relationships between principles become apparent. Those aspects of ecosystem processes that are particularly relevant to climate change adaptation or mitigation are identified in order to form an understanding of how ecosystem based biomimicry could be harnessed to address climate change and biodiversity loss impacts on the built environment. Ecosystem processes have not before been mapped to demonstrate relationships and hierarchies, p roviding an overall view of how ecosystems work, in an architectural or design context. The significance of such research is that it provides a thorough and comprehensive basis for the development of biomimicry for architecture and urban environments seeki ng to move into highly sustainable or potentially regenerative paradigms. The research presented here aims to move attempts to mimic ecosystems from the shallow, and misunderstood (Kibert et al., 2002, page 41, Paton, 1992, Korhonen, 2001) , to the more ins ightful, meaningful, and measurable levels that may be possible when knowledge from ecology and biology is thoroughly integrated into architectural design (Birkeland, 2008, page 17 , Gebeshuber et al., 2009). 3.2 ECOLOGY, ECOSYST EMS AND HUMANS
evidence_units.summary_es: Es uno de los chunks más fuertes: explica la metodología para derivar principios ecosistémicos, su representación en lista y matriz, y su relevancia para adaptación/mitigación. Puede inspirar la construcción de una matriz de criterios de factibilidad para adaptive reuse.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: alcance-limites-del-sistema-y-decision-temprana
evidence_units.original_text: Appl. Sci. 2021, 11, 5978 14 of 19 heritage values. Both for existing buildings and historic/heritage buildings, there is a need for more systematic methods for implementing and evaluating refurbishment measures, which Pracchi [23] refers to as efﬁciency diagnostics for upgrading. Such approaches will enable realizing the sustainability potential inherent in the existing building stock. 4.5. Scope, System Boundary, and Methodological Choices Performing cradle-to-grave life cycle assessment from the early design phase through the construction process and the as-built phase of the building, including all building elements as much as possible, will enable supporting informed decision making. Both Statens hus Vadsø and Bergen city hall studies were conducted in the early planning phase of the projects. Such kind of early phase studies, when knowledge is limited, will enable evaluating the potential of refurbishment in comparison to new buildings, identifying the hot spots, and implementing design strategies and energy and material efﬁciency measures during the construction and as-built phases. However, these life cycle assessments do not allow considering heritage values. In Villa Dammen, the scenario where the building was assumed to continue to operate as normal without refurbishment (without refurb.) shows the highest operational GHG emissions (97%). By refurbishing the building, up to 70% of these emissions could be saved.
evidence_units.summary_es: Plantea la conveniencia de realizar LCA cradle-to-grave desde fases tempranas y de incluir tantos elementos como sea posible para apoyar decisiones informadas. Muy pertinente para definir marco de evaluación de factibilidad.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: retos-y-necesidad-de-metodos-y-guias
evidence_units.original_text: ciency measures, and a lack of knowledge of historic and heritage buildings are considered as key challenges when implementing energy efﬁciency strategies in such buildings [23]. Nastasi and Matteo [24] argued that focusing on incorporating a renewable energy supply system instead of modiﬁcation of the building envelope or installation of a new HVAC system is a possible solution for preserving existing buildings with historic sig- niﬁcance. Studies have shown the possibilities of functional improvement in historic buildings due to technological evolutions, such as a district heating system dominated by biomass [25], integration of hydrogen into natural gas as a partial fuel substitution [ 24], and HVAC systems [26]. Development of best practice methods, tools, and guidelines with quantitative analy- ses which support qualitative study of existing buildings with historic values at a large scale (districts and cities rather than the individual building level) will enable ﬁlling in the current knowledge gap in this area [22]. Ruggeri et al. [27] developed a score-driven decision making model which can be used to plan and manage energy retroﬁtting measures applied to cultural heritage and historic buildings. The model is described as a combination of quantitative and qualitative parameters, including energy savings and costs, building conservation aspects measured as a restoration score, and their impact on indoor comfort.
evidence_units.summary_es: Sintetiza desafíos técnicos y de conocimiento al intervenir edificios con valor histórico, y pide métodos, herramientas y guías cuantitativas que apoyen estudios cualitativos. Útil para construir criterios o marcos de factibilidad.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: importancia-de-instalaciones-tecnicas-y-limites
evidence_units.original_text: This shows that adding “without refurbishment scenarios” would further support the decision-making process. The building physical system boundaries often vary but determine the emission results. The results from Statens hus Vadsø show that by including the impacts from the technical installation, there is a further increase in the embodied impacts of the new building by 67% in comparison with refurbishment (Figure 3). Moncaster et al. (2019) estimated the impact of technical installations as being between 9 and 45% of the total life cycle emissions. However, most studies excluded technical installations mainly due to a lack of data. In NS 3720, it is stated that building products that present in small quantities can be excluded, but the total excluded product within at each two-digit building element level must not exceed 5% of the total weight [37]. The LCA methods follow an internal baseline scenario approach for the reference buildings [37], where a simpliﬁed building model is developed as a reference building (using existing scenarios and generic data) in the early design phase of a project. The reference building is used as a baseline to LCA calculations during the construction and as- built phases to evaluate measures considered to improve the environmental performance of the building (such as design and energy efﬁciency strategies and material choices). This might lead to setting lower emission reduction targets based on a poor choice of background
evidence_units.summary_es: Muestra cómo la inclusión de instalaciones técnicas altera significativamente los resultados y evidencia la sensibilidad de los límites del sistema. Sirve para criterios metodológicos y lectura crítica de resultados.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: integracion_servicios_ecosistemicos_decision_urbana
evidence_units.original_text: 270 examples. Understanding ecosystem services fits well into the philosophy of encouraging people to move beyond the typical ‘reduce, recycle, efficiency’ sustainability paradigm (Reed, 2007, McDonough and Braungart, 2002) , and to grasp the possible potential for increased ecological and human health when adopting a regenerative approach. A key benefit of mimicking the functi ons of ecosystems is that through careful urban planning, buildings as part of a larger system that are able to mimic natural processes and functions in their creation, use , and eventual end of life, could have the potential to be more resilient and adapt more readily to climate change (TEEB, 2011b). The Econom ics of Ecosystems and Biodiversity group (TEEB, 2011b) provide clear reasons why an understanding of ecosystem service s should be integrated into urban level decision making. Firstly, when the benefits derived from local ecosystems become apparent and are visible to local residents these are more valued and perhaps therefore preserved. For example, understanding that purer water is a result of nearby forests in a particular city could mean it is easier to convince people of the need to conserve the forest 171. This has the potential to contribute to prioritising or preventing certain urban development projects in particular areas, and therefore to long term effect ive spatial planning, providing the overall values of society change to allow this. Secondly, the i mpacts of decision
evidence_units.summary_es: Ofrece una justificación sólida para integrar servicios ecosistémicos en la toma de decisiones urbanas y para superar paradigmas centrados solo en eficiencia. Puede fortalecer la justificación ambiental y urbana de una estrategia replicable de reconversión.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: evidencia-comparativa-reuso-vs-nueva-construccion
evidence_units.original_text: of GHG emissions, followed by replacements at 22% and end of life at 14% [40]. Existing studies also indicate the potential environmental beneﬁts from refurbishment of existing buildings in the range between 4 and 74% depending on the scenarios consid- ered in the studies. Assefa and Amber [ 45] indicated a 28–33% impact reduction from the selective deconstruction and reuse of a thirteen-story library tower at the University of Calgary, in Western Canada, in seven environmental impact categories assessed (eu- trophication potential, smog potential, global warming potential, fossil fuel consumption, human health criteria, acidiﬁcation potential, and ozone depletion). Assefa and Amber also pointed out the importance of comparative assessments for reuse vs. new construction regardless of the challenges due to the uniqueness of different buildings and their locations. Hasik et al. [46] showed 36–75% impact reductions across six environmental impact in- dicators (acidiﬁcation potential, eutrophication potential, global warming potential, ozone depletion potential, smog formation potential, and non-renewable energy demand) when refurbishment of an ofﬁce building was compared to new construction. Hasik et al. [46] also addressed the challenges related to the lack of a clear system boundary description regarding life cycle modules included in existing buildings and refurbishment projects. The study performed by Preservation Green Lab [47] on six building typologies from four
evidence_units.summary_es: Compila resultados de estudios previos que muestran reducciones de impacto al rehabilitar o reutilizar frente a construir nuevo. Aporta evidencia comparativa transferible para la justificación del enfoque de reconversión.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: estandares-lca-y-problema-de-armonizacion
evidence_units.original_text: Appl. Sci. 2021, 11, 5978 5 of 19 15897 [30] for the assessment of environmental performance of buildings, and the national Norwegian standard NS 3720 [ 31]), and in LCA results communication methods (e.g., EPDs, and green building rating systems such as BREEAM) and international research activities (such as the International Energy Agency’s Energy in Building and Communities program (IEA EBC) Annex 57 [32], Annex 72 [33]) on assessing and communicating life cycle-related impacts of buildings. Life cycle-based national GHG emission benchmarks or reference values are receiving more attention in different countries, and there are on-going discussions on the possibility of legal bindings [34]. The lack of harmonized background data and methodology choices used in different studies is considered as a major challenge in the utilization of LCA studies to establish benchmarks [34–36]. Most existing benchmarks also provide aggregated values for both new and existing buildings, mainly due to a lack of LCA studies from existing buildings and heritage buildings. As with other Nordic countries, in Norway, there is no speciﬁc legal requirements for building LCA in the national legislation yet [ 37]. However, public building owners (e.g., the Norwegian government agency for administration of public buildings in Norway, Stastbygg), large municipalities (e.g., Oslo), initiatives, and projects (e.g., Futurebuilt, FME
evidence_units.summary_es: Identifica estándares y marcos relevantes de LCA y, a la vez, el problema de la falta de armonización de datos y decisiones metodológicas. Muy útil para el marco técnico y para justificar cautela comparativa.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: justificacion-climatica-y-circularidad
evidence_units.original_text: With the growing carbon spike and climate change impacts, refurbishment and adap- tive reuse of existing buildings, and heritage buildings, can be conducive to taking imme- diate action and achieving various emission reduction goals. The EU Green Deal started a new renovation initiative in 2020, the “renovation wave”, with a target to doubling the current public and private renovation rate in the next 10 years [ 9]. Applying circularity principles would help conserve scarce raw material resources, reduce GHG emissions associated with carbon-intensive production processes (i.e., cement, steel, and glass), and boost economic crises resulting from the pandemic. The evaluation of the environmental performance of existing buildings in a life cycle perspective, using transparent and harmo- nized methods, will enable avoiding problem shifting, and evaluating and following up the fulﬁlment of emission reduction ambitions. This paper presents a summary of the main lessons learnt from the life cycle assess- ment of four Norwegian case studies to shed light on the gaps in the above-mentioned challenges and opportunities. This paper begins by outlining the state-of-the-art literature, followed by a short description of four case studies and the methodology used for eval- uating and discussing these. The LCA results of the case studies are presented, and the ﬁndings are discussed, in order to provide background for lessons learnt from each case
evidence_units.summary_es: Aporta la lógica de urgencia climática, la relación con economía circular y la necesidad de evaluar edificios existentes con métodos transparentes y armonizados. Sirve para justificar adaptive reuse frente a demolición.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: enfoque_en_funciones_y_resultados
evidence_units.original_text: environment is the motivation for incorporating an understanding of ecosystems into design, then it is th e outcomes, or functions of ecosystems, that should be the focus , not specifically how ecosystems work. Mimicking the processes of ecosystems could potentially result in better sustainability outcomes (Gebeschuber et al., 2009) but the danger exists that s uch efforts may remain at a shallow level. For example, a development that cycles matter, gathers and uses energy effectively and is able to adapt to changing conditions might be based upon an understanding of ecosystem processes. It may not have environme ntal performance outcomes that are any better overall than other ‘sustainable’ buildings or even conventional ones. Mimicking the 152 Fashion trends that result in the use of extra energy or materials and lead to waste due to demolitions to create them or when they become outmoded (Brand, 1994) rather than functionally obsolete, as is currently the case, would be prevented by a thorough ecosystem biomimicry approach because such practices would be specifically excluded from any ecosystem based and/or regenerative approach to design.
evidence_units.summary_es: Muy útil para defender que el foco de diseño debe ponerse en resultados o funciones y no solo en analogías formales o procesuales. Esto puede trasladarse a criterios de desempeño en reconversión y densificación.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: matriz_servicios_ecosistemicos
evidence_units.original_text: 230 useful in a design context for several important reasons as discussed in sections 4.6 and 4.7. The mapping of ecosystem services differs from the map ping o f ecosystem processes (figure 34 ) in two important ways. Firstly because ecosystem services relate to the values humans place on ecosystem functions, the four categories (provisioning services; regulation services; supporting services; and cultural s ervices) could not be arranged hierarchically . For these reasons the ecosystem services matrix relationship diagram is non-hierarchical in format. Although social and ecological systems are intimately linked (Berkebile and Hoxie, 2012, Folke, 2006, Health Council of the Netherlands and RMNO, 2004, Young et al., 2006, Azar et al., 1996) , and proponents of the biophilia hypothesis argue that ecosystem health both affects and is affected by human psychological health (Kellert et al., 2008), for the purposes of this research cultural services160 are thought to have less direct or obvious links to improved ecosystem health than the other categories , and therefore the actual provision of regulating, supporting and provisioning ecosystem services. Also, many of the se cultural services are not fulfilled by ecosystems exclusively. Artistic inspiration may be as equally found in a city of character as in a forest for example. Furthermore, following a ‘strong’ model of sustainability ( figure 62) where a healthy
evidence_units.summary_es: Describe diferencias metodológicas del mapeo de servicios ecosistémicos y su formato no jerárquico. Es útil para diseñar un marco multicriterio cuando no conviene jerarquizar linealmente variables arquitectónicas, regulatorias y de negocio.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: limitaciones_biomimicry_greenwashing
evidence_units.original_text: 91 technologies especially outside of the protected space of the laboratory. In some cases it might be advantageous not to follow some routes at all.’ 2.3 DRAWBACKS AND PI TFALLS OF A BIOMIMETIC DESIGN APPROACH This chapter began with a discussion of the enthusiastic but unqualified claims that biomimicry is the key to increasing the sustainability of human design products and how this could lead to biomimicry being used as a green -washing mechanism. The undefined and ‘ad hoc’ (Mathews, 2011) current approach to biomimicry is also a drawback that the framework present ed here (see section 2.5) aims to address. Aside from these two issues , there are several other potential pitfalls to using an understanding of biology or ecology in design that must be considered for it to become a useful tool for designers in the pursuit of sustainability. 2.3.1 THE ROMATICISATION OF NATURE AND EVOLUTION It is true that ecosystems and the organisms they are made of do provide an extraordinary number of examples of how living systems are energy effective, materials efficient, and extremely innovative at solving problems (Manuel, 2003) . Because many aspects of biologi cal entities as well as ecosystems are not fully understood, there is a tendency to romanticise nature as a perfect system (Vogel, 1998, page 19 , Kaplinsky, 2006) . Th is tendency is criticised by various scientists (Vincent et al., 2006, Vogel, 1998 , pages 18-19) and reinforces the need to
evidence_units.summary_es: Aporta una crítica útil sobre greenwashing, indefinición conceptual y riesgos del biomimicry. Sirve para delimitar el marco teórico y evitar extrapolaciones débiles en la propuesta.

evidence_units.source_title: Multi-Criteria Assessment for Defining Compatible New Use: Old Administrative Hospital, Kyrenia, Cyprus
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de problema
evidence_units.summary_es: La fuente sostiene que muchos edificios patrimoniales quedan obsoletos, vacantes o subutilizados, lo que acelera su deterioro, y que un problema recurrente en adaptive reuse es ignorar parte de los valores del edificio al decidir el nuevo uso. Tambien afirma que la seleccion de una funcion compatible exige considerar simultaneamente valores patrimoniales, caracteristicas fisicas y espaciales, contexto y participacion de distintos actores.

evidence_units.source_title: Multi-Criteria Assessment for Defining Compatible New Use: Old Administrative Hospital, Kyrenia, Cyprus
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de metodo
evidence_units.summary_es: El estudio propone un modelo de decision multicriterio basado en AHP. La metodologia se organiza en dos fases: 1) levantamiento de campo, revision de literatura y encuesta para definir criterios y alternativas; 2) aplicacion de AHP con comparaciones pareadas realizadas por expertos y actores locales para ponderar criterios y jerarquizar usos alternativos.

evidence_units.source_title: Multi-Criteria Assessment for Defining Compatible New Use: Old Administrative Hospital, Kyrenia, Cyprus
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de contexto
evidence_units.summary_es: El caso analizado es un antiguo hospital administrativo de Kyrenia, Chipre, edificio patrimonial del periodo britanico que perdio su funcion original, quedo mayormente vacante y requiere una nueva funcion compatible. Aunque el contexto no es Toronto ni reconversion residencial, la fuente es transferible como referencia metodologica para evaluar edificios existentes con obsolescencia funcional y valor urbano.

evidence_units.source_title: Multi-Criteria Assessment for Defining Compatible New Use: Old Administrative Hospital, Kyrenia, Cyprus
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de hallazgo
evidence_units.summary_es: La aplicacion del modelo identifico como uso mas compatible un community centre. El estudio reporta que para ambos grupos de participantes los criterios arquitectonicos tuvieron la mayor prioridad, seguidos por cultura, social, legal, ambiental y economico, y que la participacion de expertos y locales permitio una evaluacion mas comprensiva y un resultado racionalizable.

evidence_units.source_title: Multi-Criteria Assessment for Defining Compatible New Use: Old Administrative Hospital, Kyrenia, Cyprus
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de limitacion
evidence_units.summary_es: La fuente visible no presenta una seccion explicita de limitaciones. Sin embargo, el contenido sugiere dependencia del juicio de expertos y locales, asi como adaptacion de criterios y composicion del panel segun el caso, pero no formula esto como limitacion de manera directa.

evidence_units.source_title: Multi-Criteria Assessment for Defining Compatible New Use: Old Administrative Hospital, Kyrenia, Cyprus
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de linea futura
evidence_units.summary_es: La fuente indica que el modelo puede modificarse para distintos edificios, objetivos y contextos, y que la seleccion de criterios, alternativas y composicion del equipo experto puede ajustarse segun las caracteristicas de cada caso. Esto sugiere una linea futura de extension y adaptacion del metodo a otros contextos y tipologias edificatorias.

evidence_units.source_title: The adaptation and demolition of existing buildings on masterplan sites
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de problema
evidence_units.summary_es: La tesis parte de que la teoría existente sobre adaptación de edificios se concentra en el edificio individual o en la ciudad, pero resulta insuficiente para explicar decisiones en grandes sitios brownfield redearrollados mediante masterplan.

evidence_units.source_title: The adaptation and demolition of existing buildings on masterplan sites
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de metodo
evidence_units.summary_es: Se emplea un enfoque cualitativo inductivo y secuencial, con entrevistas y focus groups a profesionales del entorno construido, seguidos por estudios de caso en Cambridge, Eindhoven y Sydney para comparar cómo se toman las decisiones en distintos contextos.

evidence_units.source_title: The adaptation and demolition of existing buildings on masterplan sites
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de contexto
evidence_units.summary_es: A escala de masterplan, las decisiones sobre adaptar o demoler no dependen solo de atributos físicos del edificio; también intervienen escala relativa dentro del conjunto, flujos peatonales y vehiculares, estructura del sistema de planeamiento, condiciones económicas y poder de actores como autoridades, comunidades y desarrolladores.

evidence_units.source_title: The adaptation and demolition of existing buildings on masterplan sites
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de hallazgo
evidence_units.summary_es: Los atributos físicos siguen importando, pero la viabilidad económica se interpreta de forma distinta en grandes desarrollos porque costos y beneficios pueden compensarse en otras partes del proyecto. El patrimonio sí aparece con frecuencia, incluso como place-making, mientras que los impactos incorporados de energía y carbono rara vez se consideran en la práctica.

evidence_units.source_title: The adaptation and demolition of existing buildings on masterplan sites
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de limitacion
evidence_units.summary_es: La propia fuente reconoce que modelar la toma de decisiones como una serie de etapas puede inducir una falsa linealidad, cuando en realidad el proceso es iterativo, dinámico y con retrocesos, especialmente en contextos de masterplan.

evidence_units.source_title: The adaptation and demolition of existing buildings on masterplan sites
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de linea futura
evidence_units.summary_es: La tesis sugiere ampliar el encuadre teórico de las decisiones de adaptación/demolición mediante modelos compuestos de desarrollo urbano y mejorar el tratamiento metodológico de whole life carbon, especialmente frente a supuestos poco realistas como el reemplazo like-for-like en estudios LCA.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: diseno-metodologico-de-casos
evidence_units.original_text: Appl. Sci. 2021, 11, 5978 7 of 19 buildings in Norway, value assessments always form the basis for measures permitted by the cultural heritage authorities [12]. In Norway, the cultural heritage authorities also want buildings without formal protection to be treated in accordance with their heritage values [51]. 3. Case Studies The methodology in this study includes a quantitative review used to critically eval- uate and analyze different Norwegian case studies to provide a quantitative answer to research gaps identiﬁed under Chapter 2. Four Norwegian case studies, all refurbishment projects where LCA studies were conducted, were selected for the analysis. The calculation was performed using a functional unit of 1 m 2 and a building lifetime of 60 years. LCA practitioners have used either klimagassregnskap.no (KGR.no, the previous Norwegian GHG emission calculation tool, using the Ecoinvent database and EPDs as background data), SimaPro (commercial LCA software using the ecoinvent database), OneClick LCA (commercial LCA tool which replaced KGR.no in 2018), or the ZEB tool (Excel-based GHG emission calculation tool developed by the Norwegian ZEB Centre, using the Ecoinvent database and EPDs as background data) for GHG emission calculations. The calculations follow the LCA methodology outlined by LCA standards, NS 3720, EN 15978, and/or ISO 14040/44. The Norwegian case studies consist of one residential building: Villa Dammen, and
evidence_units.summary_es: Describe el diseño metodológico del estudio de casos, la base cuantitativa, los cuatro casos y parámetros como unidad funcional y vida útil. Es útil para inspirar una metodología aplicada comparativa.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: limitacion-sobre-valores-patrimoniales-en-lca
evidence_units.original_text: three ofﬁce buildings: Powerhouse Kjørbo, Bergen city hall, and Statens bygg Vadsø, bygg B. Villa Dammen, Bergen city hall, and Statens bygg Vadsø, bygg B (herein referred to as Statens bygg Vadsø) are buildings with heritage signiﬁcance. The heritage values were not considered directly in the LCA model when assessing the life cycle analysis as this type of value is not possible to consider in the existing models. However, heritage values were considered when choosing measures to reduce emission values. Powerhouse Kjørbo, an ofﬁce building from the 1980s, was selected as a case study as it was the ﬁrst refurbishment project in the world which produces more energy than it consumes. The summary of the general information about the case studies and the results from the life cycle assessment is shown in Table 1.
evidence_units.summary_es: Expone una limitación relevante: los valores patrimoniales no se incorporan directamente en los modelos LCA actuales, aunque sí influyen en la selección de medidas. Muy útil para delimitar alcances de evaluación.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: incertidumbre-de-datos-y-efecto-caso-especifico
evidence_units.original_text: Appl. Sci. 2021, 11, 5978 15 of 19 Kjørbo was based on some assumptions, whilst in Statens hus Vadsø and Bergen city hall, the calculations were based on the background data from OneClick LCA. The data from OneClick LCA, especially for the construction phase, are described as uncertain and to be lacking transparency [ 37]. As the results of LCA are dependent on the background data, using transparent background data is useful. The LCA studies performed based on actual construction data collected from Norwegian construction sites can be used as an example [64,66,68]. The results exhibit the effect of case-speciﬁc factors, such as refurbishment measures, and methodological choices on GHG emission reductions. Conducting comprehensive LCA studies will enable identifying environmentally preferable refurbishment measures. However, heritage value considerations are most often lacking in such studies. 5. Future Research Perspectives There are several aspects that need to be covered in future research activities address- ing the role of existing buildings. • Even if improving the energy efﬁciency performance of historic/heritage buildings is challenging, a thorough assessment is needed. Setting ambitious refurbishment targets to fulﬁl the current energy performance regulation as minimum ambitions, focusing on achieving zero emissions or plus house by incorporating energy efﬁciency and renewable energy measures, is important. The uncertainties of future energy mixes and
evidence_units.summary_es: Detalla la incertidumbre de las bases de datos y cómo los resultados dependen de factores específicos del caso y de elecciones metodológicas. Útil para justificar una metodología prudente y comparativa, no concluyente.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: diversidad_relaciones_y_resiliencia
evidence_units.original_text:  Increased opportunities for humans to interact with and possibly begin to restore local ecosystems. Brand (1994), Folke, 2006), Kibert et al. (2002), Newman et al. (2009), Portland Development Commission (2004). 4. Ecosystems are diverse in species, relationships and information.  Increase diversity to increase resilience.  Create and foster a variety of relationships in the development and with groups outside it.  Utilise opportunities to create self-organising and distributed systems.  Translating this ecosystem process into the built environment implies a systems approach to architectural design where the facilitation of relationships between buildings, components, people and ecosystems is as important as designing the individual buildings themselves.  More robust built environment and community able to adapt to climate change.  Decisions based on a broader knowledge base are likely to be more sustainable (Wahl and Baxter, 2008). Berkebile and Hoxie (2012), Cole et al. (2012), Hoxie et al. (2012), Plaut et al. (2012), Svec et al. (2012), Gifford (2007), Walker at al. (2004), Moffatt and Kohler (2008), Pickett et al. (2008), Alberti et al. (2003). Leaman et al. (2010), Francis (1999). 5. Ecosystems are self- organising, decentralised and distributed.  Decision making could become more localised and independent to reflect local changes and conditions.  Power generation and distribution may become more decentralised.
evidence_units.summary_es: Aporta una traducción de procesos ecosistémicos a estrategias de diseño sistémico, enfatizando diversidad, relaciones y resiliencia. Es útil para criterios de evaluación arquitectónica y urbana de soluciones replicables.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: complementariedad_con_otras_estrategias
evidence_units.original_text: 217 such as: permaculture (Co peman, 2008); bioclimatic design (Swaid et al., 1993, Yeang, 1994) ; vernacular design (Oliver, 1997) ; participatory or integrated design (Francis, 1999) ; post occupancy evaluation techniques (Leaman et al., 2010) ; regenerative design strategies to develop a sense of place (Hoxie et al., 2012, Mang and Reed, 2012, Reed, 2007) ; and local ecology/geography/climate modelling (Dymond and Shepherd, 2004, Mathieu et al., 2007). This is not to say that some examples of individual organism based biomimetic technologies do not have significant improved sustaina bility outcomes (section 2.7 ), but so do non -biomimetic sustainable technologies. The issue is not that humans need new te chnologies to solve problems regarding climate change and the loss of biodiversity (amo ng other serious environmental problems) but rather tha t people need to apply what has already been developed , and reassess their consumption behaviour and relationship to ecosystems, so that the idea of sustainability becomes physically manifest in the built environment. 3.8 CONCLUSIONS: CHAPTER THREE In this chapter a theoretical framework for the use of ecosystem biomimicry at the process level was devised and ecosystem processes were explored for their potential to become part of an understanding of ec ology that could contribute to architectural or urban design. Key findings are that more work has been done in terms of
evidence_units.summary_es: Ayuda a no sobredimensionar biomimicry como solución única y a integrarlo con diseño bioclimático, evaluación post-ocupación, diseño participativo y estrategias regenerativas. Sirve para un marco técnico equilibrado.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments

evidence_units.original_text: 54 This would at the same time restore or create ecosystem services and add to resilience (Chapin et al., 2000, Rands et al., 2010). Many current climate change mitigation and adaptation strategies can be described as ‘sustainable’. This is an impr ovement on design that does not consider environmental impacts at all however ‘sustainable’ design ultimately still results in negative environmental impact (Reed, 2007) . Through incremental steps the ultimate goal of such an approach is neutral environmen tal impact as demonstrated in figure 6. Until this goal is reached, the built environment continues to degrade the ecosystems and climate humans are dependent upon for well - being, wealth, and basic survival . Under many current definitions s ustainable design seeks to minimise pollution rather than achieving clean air, soil and water. It minimises energy use, rather than us ing energy from non -damaging renewable sources. It minimises waste rather than eliminating it altogether by creating positive cycles of re source use (McDonough and Braungart, 2002 , page 62 ). Within this paradigm, no attempt is made to remediate past damage. Most urban environments are built in such a way that th e outcome is detrimental to climate, ecosystems, and to people (Newman, 2006), rather than nearing even approximate ‘sustainability’, even if the original meaning of the term (to continue indefinitely) is implied. The goals of creating neutral environmental outcomes in terms of energy use,
evidence_units.summary_es: Útil para justificar por qué no basta con minimizar impactos y por qué conviene un enfoque regenerativo. Puede apoyar la justificación ambiental general de la tesis, aunque no trata adaptive reuse de forma específica.

evidence_units.original_text: 95 The issues explored in this section do not mean that human designers cannot learn anything from studying how biology or ecosystems work to become more able to live within them without negative effects, but findings suggest that there is a need both to explore and respect solutions seen in the living world along with tested and successful strategies that humans have devised , and to understand the differences between them. This is important in order to make decisions about what is and is not appropriate in terms of design , so that sustainability is increased , and so that the greater philosophical or ethical implications of biomimicry -for-sustainability are not ignored. 2.4 APPROACHES TO EM PLOYING BIOMIMICRY Through a comparative literature review, and an examination of existing biomimetic technologies, this section defines distinct approaches to biomi metic design. As part of using a critical theory research methodology, terms for these distinctive approaches have been established. Approaches to biomimicry as a design process typically fall into two categories. The first is termed here: ‘design referencing biology’. This is when a human need or design problem is defined first, and then the ways other organisms or ecosystems solve this is explored. The second approach is termed ‘biology influencing design’. This refers to biomimicry where a particular characteristic, behaviour or function in an organism or ecosystem is identified, and is
evidence_units.summary_es: Indica que la autora define enfoques distintos de biomimicry mediante revisión comparada de literatura y tecnologías existentes. Es útil para respaldar una estrategia de análisis comparado de precedentes y marcos conceptuales.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: prioridad_biomimicry_ecosistema
evidence_units.original_text: 145 3.0 CHAPTER THREE: E XAMINING ECOSYSTEM P ROCESS ES FOR A BUILT ENVIRONMENT CO NTEXT ‘It is clear that industrial systems and biologic al systems are deeply similar. I ndeed it is clear that both are complex systems, and that in ge neral, they evolve to maintain themselves over time without discontinuous change… C onsiderable care must be taken not to let attractive similarities blind one to significant differences, especially where an understanding of those differences can be equally valuable.’ (Allenby and C ooper, 1994) . 3.1 INTRODUCTION Mimicking ecosystems, rather than aspects of individual organisms , is important in biomimicry that seeks to increase the sustainability of the built environment and contribute to a regenerative de sign paradigm. In chapter two it was established that ecosystem biomimicry at the process or function level may have greater potential for long term effective GHG mitigation and adaptation to climate change impacts, while enabling the built environment to become an agent of ecosystem regeneration and a provider of ecosystem services. This chapter will explore this finding further and will build upon the framework presented in chapter two, by examining and defining ecosystem biomimicry at the process level i n greater depth. Ecosystem biomimicry at the function level will be examined in chapter four. This and the subsequent chapter aim to provide an integrated set of principles that could form the theoretical
evidence_units.summary_es: Sostiene que imitar ecosistemas, más que organismos individuales, tiene mayor potencial para un paradigma regenerativo. Es útil para el marco teórico de sostenibilidad urbana sistémica.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: metodologia_argumentacion_logica
evidence_units.original_text: 66 A logical argumentation project is often set within a larger ‘transcendental venue’ such as ‘nature’, ‘the machine’, or ‘morality’ (Groat a nd Wang, 2002 , page 303 ). This thesis sees ‘nature’ as a system or model which works well within the planet’s ecological operational limitations and functions in a way which is potentially useful for human s to learn from . The logical groundwork for living within ecosystems without degrading them and regenerating them is assumed to be found within understanding ecosystems and then translating this knowledge into a human context. This philosophical stance is discussed further in section 2.2.4. 1.7.2 STAGES OF LOGICAL ARGUMENTATION THROUGHOUT THE THESIS A logical argumentation approach implies that the project has the following traits: broad systemic applicability, paradigmatic innovation, and testability (Groat and Wang, 2002, pages 308-311). The rest of the thesis is divided in to parts following these traits66. Part one of the thesis is a primary logical argumentation treatise because it argues for a new way of conceiving the built environment (broad systemic applicability) and then provides analytic framewo rks and matrix tools (paradigmatic innovation). Primary logical systems can be described as ‘innovative’ because they work at the level of paradigm discourse. A paradigm exists within a cultural and historical context, so most primary logical systems have connections to previously expressed
evidence_units.summary_es: Describe explícitamente el enfoque metodológico de argumentación lógica y su marco filosófico. Es valioso como referencia para estructurar una metodología conceptual aplicada en arquitectura.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de problema
evidence_units.summary_es: La fuente plantea que la mayor parte del parque edificado seguirá en uso hacia 2050 y que la demolición y nueva construcción no son la única vía para cumplir metas climáticas; sin embargo, existen pocas evaluaciones LCA de edificios existentes con valor patrimonial y hay falta de datos de fondo y decisiones metodológicas armonizadas.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de metodo
evidence_units.summary_es: El estudio aplica una revisión de literatura de LCA y un análisis cuantitativo de cuatro casos noruegos de rehabilitación, usando una perspectiva de ciclo de vida y comparando escenarios de rehabilitación y nueva construcción con unidad funcional por m2 y vida útil definida.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de contexto
evidence_units.summary_es: El artículo se enmarca en las metas climáticas del Acuerdo de París, el Green Deal europeo y la necesidad de reducir emisiones del sector edificatorio, resaltando además el rol de la circularidad y de la conservación de recursos materiales y valores culturales.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de hallazgo
evidence_units.summary_es: La señal principal es que prolongar la vida útil mediante rehabilitación y adaptive reuse ofrece potencial significativo para reducir emisiones de GEI, ahorrar recursos materiales y mantener valores culturales; además, varios resultados comparativos muestran menores emisiones incorporadas frente a escenarios de obra nueva.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de limitacion
evidence_units.summary_es: La fuente subraya limitaciones importantes: escasez de estudios LCA sobre edificios existentes y patrimoniales, variación caso por caso en medidas de intervención, incertidumbre en datos de fondo, falta de transparencia metodológica y dificultad para incorporar valores socioculturales y patrimoniales en los modelos actuales.

evidence_units.source_title: How Can Existing Buildings with Historic Values Contribute to Achieving Emission Reduction Ambitions?
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de linea futura
evidence_units.summary_es: Propone estudios LCA más holísticos que cubran todo el ciclo de vida e integren dimensiones económicas, sociales y patrimoniales, junto con análisis de escenarios, métodos sistemáticos de evaluación y mejores prácticas comparables para apoyar decisiones de rehabilitación frente a demolición y obra nueva.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de problema
evidence_units.summary_es: La fuente plantea que las respuestas convencionales del entorno construido frente al cambio climático y la pérdida de biodiversidad son insuficientes y fragmentadas; por ello propone marcos que integren mitigación, adaptación y restauración con resultados ambientales positivos.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de metodo
evidence_units.summary_es: La investigación usa una argumentación lógica apoyada en revisión de literatura, construcción de principios generalizados, matrices de procesos y servicios ecosistémicos, y prueba de aplicabilidad en un caso urbano existente.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de contexto
evidence_units.summary_es: Se sitúa en el debate sobre crisis climática, degradación ecosistémica y responsabilidad del entorno construido como principal sitio de actividad cultural y económica, con énfasis en contextos urbanos.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de hallazgo
evidence_units.summary_es: La tesis sostiene que el biomimicry a nivel de ecosistema y el uso de servicios ecosistémicos ofrecen una base más robusta para redefinir metas de desempeño ambiental y localizar puntos de apalancamiento para el cambio en el entorno urbano construido.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de limitacion
evidence_units.summary_es: La propia fuente reconoce limitaciones de definición y evidencia cuantitativa en el diseño regenerativo y advierte riesgos del biomimicry ad hoc o superficial, además de señalar límites de alcance y de transferencia directa entre sistemas biológicos y diseño humano.

evidence_units.source_title: Ecosystem Services Analysis for the Design of Regenerative Urban Built Environments
evidence_units.label: Senal interpretativa de linea futura
evidence_units.summary_es: Se infiere como línea futura la necesidad de mayor prueba empírica, monitoreo de ejemplos construidos y desarrollo de aplicaciones prácticas en diseño urbano y arquitectónico basadas en servicios ecosistémicos y biomimicry de ecosistemas.

evidence_units.source_title: Intake del proyecto
evidence_units.label: Contexto declarado por el investigador
evidence_units.original_text:
Tema: Ideas de negocio para contribuir a superar la crisis de vivienda en Canadá mediante la sobre-elevación en mass timber como estrategia de adaptive reuse en edificios comerciales tipo B y C en Toronto.
Contexto/problema: La crisis de vivienda en Canadá obliga a las ciudades a identificar estrategias de densificación y reconversión de activos subutilizados con mayor rapidez y menor huella de carbono. En Toronto, numerosos edificios comerciales tipo B y C presentan vacancia, obsolescencia funcional y baja rentabilidad, pero podrían convertirse en soporte para soluciones de adaptive reuse y sobre-elevación liviana en mass timber si se demuestra su viabilidad arquitectónica, normativa y de negocio.
Linea de investigacion: Arquitectura sostenible, adaptive reuse, regeneración urbana y factibilidad de reconversión habitacional.
Poblacion o ambito: Edificios comerciales tipo B y C en Toronto, en condiciones de obsolescencia o subutilización, con potencial de reconversión habitacional y sobre-elevación en mass timber mediante estrategias de adaptive reuse.
Datos disponibles: Se asume disponibilidad de tipologías de edificios comerciales obsoletos, precedentes de reconversión de oficinas a vivienda, referencias sobre extensiones verticales en mass timber, criterios de zoning y building code de Toronto, indicadores de demanda de vivienda y marcos de factibilidad arquitectónica y económica.
Metodologia preferida: Enfoque aplicado con análisis tipológico, revisión comparada de precedentes, construcción de criterios de evaluación para adaptive reuse y sobre-elevación en mass timber, y formulación de un marco de factibilidad arquitectónica, regulatoria y de negocio para la reconversión habitacional.
Notas del asesor: Mantener el foco en adaptive reuse, la reconversión comercial-residencial y la sobre-elevación en mass timber como estrategia replicable frente a la crisis de vivienda. Priorizar criterios de selección tipológica, compatibilidad programática, restricciones normativas, viabilidad económica preliminar y potencial de densificación. Evitar convertir la tesis en un proyecto ejecutivo completo.
evidence_units.summary_es: Contexto de partida aportado por el investigador; no debe citarse como fuente bibliografica.

methodology_decision_packet.method_family: MCDM aplicado con base cualitativa-comparada y validacion experta
methodology_decision_packet.research_design: Diseno secuencial mixto de alcance aplicado: revision sintetica del corpus -> analisis tipologico y de precedentes -> construccion de criterios -> ponderacion multicriterio preliminar (AHP o scoring ponderado) -> validac
candidate_techniques: Matriz de extraccion de criterios desde literatura priorizada.
Analisis tipologico de edificios comerciales B y C candidatos.
Revision comparada de precedentes de conversion/adaptive reuse y ampliacion vertical.
Construccion de matriz de factibilidad por dimensiones: arquitectonica, regulatoria, ambiental y de negocio.
AHP con comparaciones pareadas para ponderar criterios, sujeto a panel experto.
Scoring multicriterio simplificado cuando no sea viable implementar AHP completo.
Entrevistas semiestructuradas o panel Delphi corto con expertos locales para validar pesos y umbrales.
Analisis de escenarios para alternativas de intervencion: reuse parcial, conversion completa, conversion + sobre-elevacion.
selection_rationale: El corpus ofrece apoyo directo a los modelos multicriterio y a la estructuracion de decisiones complejas con criterios heterogeneos.
Tambien ofrece apoyo fuerte a un enfoque secuencial e iterativo, mas coherente con la naturaleza no lineal de estas decisiones.
Permite traducir el problema de tesis a una herramienta util para decision preliminar, lo cual tiene alta utilidad academica y profesional.
No exige afirmar causalidad ni contar con base de datos local completa desde el inicio; puede operar como marco tentativo que luego se valida.
Es compatible con el interes del proyecto por criterios de evaluacion, factibilidad y formulacion de ideas de negocio.
missing_validations: Validacion estructural específica para sobre-elevacion en mass timber sobre edificios comerciales existentes.
Validacion economico-financiera con supuestos de mercado local.
Validacion del panel de criterios y ponderaciones con actores locales.
Prueba piloto en casos reales o prototipos locales con datos consistentes.

framework_decision_packet.core_framework: Marco de factibilidad multicriterio para adaptive reuse sostenible sensible a vacancia/subuso
supporting_frameworks: Tipologia de intervencion de adaptive reuse: WBAR, MUML, PAR y STAR.
Whole-life thinking / LCA como modulo ambiental de apoyo, no como evidencia concluyente unica.
Marco multiescalar de decision: edificio-activo-conjunto urbano.
Servicios ecosistemicos/regenerative design como modulo optativo de valor urbano-ambiental.
selection_rationale: El framework de W4376279888 es el mas cercano al problema porque incorpora vacancia/subuso y tipos de reuse poco atendidos por la literatura.
El marco de W3127169891 aporta una logica operativa para pasar de criterios abstractos a seleccion/ponderacion transparente.
W3013726001 ayuda a evitar simplificaciones lineales y a reconocer que los criterios cambian segun escala, actor y contexto de desarrollo.
W3177188553 fortalece la justificacion ambiental y la cautela metodologica respecto a carbono y comparaciones con obra nueva.
W4200361813 puede enriquecer el discurso de valor urbano y regenerativo, pero no conviene sobredimensionarlo metodologicamente en esta tesis.
missing_validations: Aterrizaje del framework a edificios comerciales no patrimoniales B y C en Toronto.
Incorporacion explicita de modulo regulatorio local.
Incorporacion explicita de modulo de negocio con indicadores de mercado y riesgo.
Validacion empírica de pesos, umbrales y reglas de descarte.

asset_usage_plan.usage_reason: Sustentar una tabla propia sobre tipos de adaptive reuse segun extension de la intervencion.
handling_notes: Usar solo como referencia de clasificacion.
Explicar textualmente cada tipo y su relevancia para edificios subutilizados.
No copiar sin reinterpretacion para el caso de tesis.

asset_usage_plan.usage_reason: Base para una tabla de factores y herramientas de decision a lo largo del ciclo de vida del edificio.
handling_notes: Reescribir la tabla en formato sintetico.
Destacar la ausencia de vacancia como variable explicita en varias herramientas.
Vincularla con la propuesta de criterios de tesis.

asset_usage_plan.usage_reason: Apoyar una figura o tabla sobre timing de intervencion en adaptive reuse.
handling_notes: Enfatizar la diferencia entre intervenir en subuso creciente versus fin de vida.
Conectar con la oportunidad de reconversion temprana en stock comercial obsoleto.

asset_usage_plan.usage_reason: Base directa para derivar familias de criterios multicriterio.
handling_notes: Adaptar criterios patrimoniales al contexto comercial/residencial cuando corresponda.
No asumir que el peso relativo del caso de Chipre es transferible a Toronto.
Explicar cuales criterios se conservan, fusionan o eliminan.

asset_usage_plan.usage_reason: Sustentar la explicacion tecnica del control de consistencia en AHP.

inferable_with_care: Que un instrumento multicriterio es apropiado para evaluar compatibilidad y prefactibilidad de reuse cuando intervienen criterios heterogeneos.
Que la vacancia/subuso debe modelarse como gradiente o condicion diferenciada y no como supuesto binario.
Que los criterios deben agruparse al menos en dimensiones arquitectonicas, regulatorias/legales, ambientales y economicas/de negocio.
Que la decision debe evaluarse por escenarios de intervencion, no solo por una opcion unica.
blocking_gaps: Parametros normativos de Toronto para conversion a vivienda y sobre-elevacion.
Datos estructurales y de capacidad de edificios objetivo.
Datos de mercado para el modelo de negocio.
Precedentes locales verificables de mass timber overbuild en edificios comerciales existentes.
validation_actions: Recuperar y rehidratar fuentes locales de zoning by-law, Ontario Building Code/Toronto requirements y guias de office-to-residential conversion.
Incorporar una ola LLM o revision experta dedicada exclusivamente a mass timber additions y requisitos de fire/structure/acoustics/egress.
Construir una ficha de caso-tipo local con variables minimas: ano, luces, modulación, altura libre, sistema estructural, ocupacion, vacancia, lote, zoning.
Realizar 5-10 entrevistas expertas o Delphi corto para validar criterios, pesos y umbrales de descarte.
Preparar una prefactibilidad economica con escenarios y rangos, no con cifras puntuales no verificadas.
Si es posible, aplicar el marco a 1-3 prototipos de edificios para probar consistencia y utilidad.
do_not_claim: No afirmar que la sobre-elevacion en mass timber ya es viable en Toronto sin validacion local.
No afirmar que adaptive reuse siempre reduce carbono frente a obra nueva; el corpus muestra dependencia de escenarios y limites del sistema.
No afirmar consenso universal sobre pesos de criterios; la evidencia muestra variacion entre actores y contextos.
No usar el contexto del intake como cita academica.
No presentar el marco regenerativo como evidencia empírica suficiente de factibilidad de negocio.

usage_notes_es: Los pasos 7-11 deben leer este artefacto como insumo de partida, no volver a recuperar PDFs.
Las unidades con citation_eligibility=context_only no son citas bibliograficas.
Las senales interpreted_signal orientan redaccion, pero las citas deben priorizar original_excerpt o asset_reference.
Los assets con ruta local pueden renderizarse mediante /api/blueprint-launch/assets?path=...

checks.message: Todos los claims mantienen IDs de evidencia existentes.
Todas las secciones tienen unidades de evidencia o contexto.
Todas las imagenes seleccionadas tienen ruta local legible.
No quedan secciones con readiness baja.
Auditoria LLM encontro 9 claim(s) debiles o sin soporte.

unsupported_claims: Cualquier afirmacion de que la sobre-elevacion en mass timber ya es viable en Toronto carece de evidencia local verificable en el corpus visible.

Cualquier afirmacion concluyente sobre cumplimiento de zoning, Ontario Building Code, fire code, egress, permisos o politicas de conversion residencial en Toronto/Canada no esta soportada por IDs del paquete.
Cualquier afirmacion de factibilidad estructural para agregar niveles en madera masiva sobre edificios comerciales B/C carece de evidencia especifica del corpus para ese contexto.
Cualquier afirmacion de viabilidad economico-financiera local, demanda, rentas, absorcion, CAPEX/OPEX o modelo de negocio especifico para Toronto no esta soportada por evidencia del paquete.
Cualquier afirmacion de precedentes locales verificables de office/commercial-to-residential con sobre-elevacion en madera masiva en Toronto no esta soportada por IDs presentes.
Cualquier afirmacion de consenso universal o pesos transferibles de criterios AHP/MCDM al caso Toronto no esta soportada; la evidencia disponible apunta a variacion por actor y contexto.
Cualquier uso del intake o de snippets tipo *-intake como cita academica para sustentar el argumento principal seria un claim sin evidencia bibliografica valida.

Cualquier afirmacion de que adaptive reuse siempre reduce carbono frente a obra nueva es excesiva respecto del soporte disponible, que depende de limites del sistema, escenarios y comparabilidad metodologica.
Presentar el marco regenerativo/servicios ecosistemicos como nucleo empiricamente validado de factibilidad excede el soporte del corpus visible.
traceability_warnings: La seccion case_context depende fuertemente de signals e intake:project-context; eso sirve para encuadre del caso, pero no para citas academicas ni para claims factuales locales.
Hay mezcla de evidence units elegibles como direct_quote con signals paraphrase_only. En redaccion, los signals deben orientar sintesis, no reemplazar citas primarias.

Existen snippets tipo *-intake con citation_eligibility direct_quote, pero por regla del propio paquete no deben usarse como cita academica; tratarlos como metadato operativo, no como evidencia sustantiva.

En evaluation_criteria hay apoyo importante en assets/equations/tables. Eso permite trazabilidad estructural, pero exige explicacion textual propia y no transferencia automatica de pesos o resultados.

Los assets con citation_eligibility asset_reference son adecuados para tablas/figuras de apoyo, pero no deben cargar por si solos claims interpretativos complejos sin acompanamiento de excerpts originales.
Algunas secciones marcadas como readiness alta estan bien para redaccion de literatura y marco metodologico, pero no para cerrar conclusiones aplicadas al caso Toronto.
La trazabilidad de hallazgos ambientales debe redactarse con cautela metodologica por la presencia explicita de limitaciones LCA y problemas de armonizacion/like-for-like.
La presencia de una 'referencia Toronto' en un excerpt de W3127169891 no equivale a validacion local integral del caso ni reemplaza corpus normativo local.
handoff_notes: Redactar cada seccion separando visualmente: evidencia directa del corpus, inferencias prudentes y vacios pendientes. No mezclar estos niveles en un mismo parrafo sin marcarlos.
Para antecedentes, problema, justificacion y marcos, priorizar snippets original_excerpt antes que signals; usar signals solo para ordenar la narrativa.
En case_context, declarar expresamente que el contexto Toronto proviene del planteamiento del investigador y requiere validacion externa posterior; no citar intake como fuente academica.
En metodologia, formular el estudio como marco preliminar/aplicado de factibilidad multicriterio, no como demostracion de viabilidad real del caso.
En evaluation_criteria, adaptar criterios de W3127169891 y W3013726001 al caso de tesis, pero explicitar que los pesos no son transferibles y quedan sujetos a validacion local.
Tratar mass timber rooftop addition como alternativa a evaluar dentro de escenarios, no como solucion validada.
Mantener una seccion o subapartado de limitaciones desde el inicio, incluyendo ausencia de normativa local, datos estructurales y datos de mercado.
Si se usan tablas/figuras derivadas de assets, reescribirlas y etiquetar claramente su fuente base por ID, evitando copia literal no interpretada.
No redactar afirmaciones normativas, estructurales o financieras especificas de Toronto hasta que el siguiente lab rehidrate corpus local y precedentes pertinentes.
Preparar para el siguiente lab una lista de claims que quedaran en modo 'por validar localmente': regulatorio, estructural, mercado, precedentes y validacion experta.
Conviene que el siguiente lab construya una matriz claim->IDs->eligibilidad para los apartados sensibles, especialmente case_context, methodology aplicada y evaluation_criteria.

Antes de cerrar borradores, revisar que toda frase con verbos fuertes como 'demuestra', 'confirma', 'viabiliza', 'cumple' o 'garantiza' tenga soporte en original_excerpt o asset pertinente y no solo en signal.

hydration_notes_es: Las optimizaciones del Paso 6 solo compactan prompts; no eliminan PDFs, texto plano, chunks, assets ni evidence_units.
Las olas LLM posteriores deben usar section_dossiers como indice y rehidratar texto completo desde sourceChunksPath, extractedTextPath y asset_path.
Las unidades interpreted_signal orientan lectura, pero las citas deben salir de original_excerpt o asset_reference.
